things.name: Bottcherstrasse
things.description: Extraordinary Art Nouveau and Expressionist alley built by coffee merchant Ludwig Roselius in the 1920s; a UNESCO-nominated lane of gilded facades and unique museums

things.name: Schnoorviertel
things.description: Bremen's perfectly preserved medieval fishermen's quarter of 15th and 16th-century gabled houses, now full of artisan craft shops, galleries and cafes

things.name: Bremen Market Square and Roland Statue
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage market square with the 1404 Roland statue, the ornate Renaissance Town Hall and the stunning Gothic St. Peter's Cathedral

things.name: Bremen Town Musicians Statue
things.description: The beloved 1953 Gerhard Marcks sculpture of the Brothers Grimm Town Musicians of Bremen; rub the donkey's hooves for good luck

things.name: Kunsthalle Bremen
things.description: One of Germany's most important art museums with a superb collection of French Impressionists, Dutch Old Masters and German Romanticism

things.name: Universum Science Centre
things.description: Striking shell-shaped science museum with 300 interactive exhibits exploring the human body, nature and the cosmos; one of Germany's top science venues

things.name: Wallanlagen Park
things.description: Beautiful parkland following the moat of Bremen's medieval walls with mature trees, windmills, a rose garden and the Stadtgraben canal

things.name: Weser Promenade (Schlachte)
things.description: Lively riverside promenade along the Weser with outdoor bars, restaurants and boat trips; the historic loading quay of the Hanseatic city

things.name: Weser Stadium (wohninvest WESERSTADION)
things.description: Home of Werder Bremen with a 42,000 capacity on the Weser riverbank; the Green-Whites have won four Bundesliga titles and the 1992 UEFA Cup

things.name: Focke Museum
things.description: Bremen's regional history museum in a park setting with extensive collections on the city's Hanseatic trading history and maritime heritage
